hooks_predicted: Did you know that Xi'an is not just home to the Terracotta Warriors, but also one of China's most exciting food destinations?
Stick around as we count down the 20 must-try street foods in Xi'an
trust me, you won't want to miss number 1.
selling_points_predicted: One of China's most exciting food destinations beyond the famous Terracotta Warriors
Ancient Silk Road city offering a street food paradise with 20 must-try dishes
From hand-pulled noodles to crispy lamb skewers showcasing diverse culinary traditions
Lao Liu Hulatang opens as early as 6am with locals flocking for authentic breakfast
Fanji Rugiamo serves hand-shredded meat in freshly-baked fire-grilled flatbread for perfect crisp-to-tender ratio
Cooling Liangpi cold noodles perfect for Xi'an's hot summers
Jia San Steamed Bun Shop with over 100 years of history serving traditional soup dumplings
First Noodle Under the Sun draws large crowds for iconic hand-pulled Biang Biang Noodles
Lao Sun Jia offers traditional and authentic Yang Rou Pao Mou experience as one of the oldest establishments
Rich lamb stew slow-cooked for hours creating deeply flavorful and aromatic broth
Muslim Quarter Night Market vendors sell freshly steamed desserts and fried snacks to order
Generous portions and affordable prices at family-run local eateries like Huashan Xiaoqi Dian
